anovapowersim 1.1.0.9000
  • Get started

  • Changelog

Specify a within-subject covariance structure

Source: R/within_covariance.R
within_covariance.Rd

Creates a readable covariance specification for power_n() and power_curve(). Supply default standard deviations and correlations, then override only the individual measurements or pairs that differ. Measurement names are generated from the within design; for example, within = c(time = 2, condition = 2) creates time1_condition1, time1_condition2, time2_condition1, and time2_condition2.

Usage

within_covariance(
  default_sd = 1,
  default_correlation = 0.5,
  standard_deviations = NULL,
  correlations = NULL
)

Arguments

default_sd

Positive finite standard deviation used for measurements not listed in standard_deviations.

default_correlation

Finite correlation in (-1, 1) used for pairs not listed in correlations.

standard_deviations

Optional named numeric vector of measurement- specific standard deviations. Names must match cells generated by the within design.

correlations

Optional named numeric vector of pair-specific correlations. Name each pair as "cell1:cell2", for example "time1:time2" = 0.7. Pair order does not matter.

Value

An anovapowersim_covariance_spec object. power_n() and power_curve() resolve it against their within design and construct the covariance matrix as D R D, where D contains the standard deviations and R is the correlation matrix. They also derive the tested term's population Greenhouse–Geisser epsilon from the resolved matrix and apply it to power_calc.

Examples

covariance <- within_covariance(
  default_sd = 1,
  default_correlation = 0.5,
  standard_deviations = c(
    "time3_condition2" = 1.2
  ),
  correlations = c(
    "time1_condition1:time1_condition2" = 0.6,
    "time2_condition1:time2_condition2" = 0.7
  )
)

# \donttest{
power_n(
  within = c(time = 3, condition = 2),
  term = "time:condition",
  target_pes = 0.14,
  power = 0.90,
  n_sims = 100,
  covariance = covariance,
  seed = 123
)
#> Warning: `power_sim` and `power_calc` differ by more than 5 percentage points for n_per_cell = 47 (largest difference = 0.058). Try increasing `n_sims` for a more stable simulation estimate.
#> <anovapowersim_curve>
#>   term:          'time:condition'
#>   target power:  0.900
#>   alpha:         0.05
#>   effect size:   pes = 0.14
#>   n values:      7 per-cell sample sizes visited
#>   sims per cell size: 100
#>   epsilon:       0.955892
#>   covariance:    custom 6 x 6 within-subject matrix
#>   SS type:       III
#>   n needed for between-subjects cell: 48
#>   total N needed: 48
#> 
#>  n_per_cell total_n n_sims  epsilon   num_df    den_df    ncp power_calc
#>          34      34    100 0.955892 1.911784  63.08887 10.270      0.813
#>          46      46    100 0.955892 1.911784  86.03028 14.005      0.922
#>          47      47    100 0.955892 1.911784  87.94207 14.316      0.928
#>          48      48    100 0.955892 1.911784  89.85385 14.627      0.934
#>          49      49    100 0.955892 1.911784  91.76564 14.939      0.939
#>          53      53    100 0.955892 1.911784  99.41277 16.183      0.955
#>          68      68    100 0.955892 1.911784 128.08953 20.852      0.987
#>  power_sim
#>      0.780
#>      0.890
#>      0.870
#>      0.940
#>      0.930
#>      0.970
#>      1.000
# }
